description: Clears children inside standard user cache folders without removing the folders themselves.
Removes npm cache, logs, and temporary npx cache.
Removes Yarn package caches.
Removes rebuildable Gradle caches and worker state.
Removes pnpm store cache.
Removes rebuildable Xcode DerivedData.
Removes CoreSimulator caches.
Runs Homebrew cleanup or clears Homebrew cache children in Trash mode.

paths: Homebrew cache, logs, Cellar, Caskroom, and var folders
description: Review-only Homebrew storage inventory with brew cleanup guidance.

paths: Documents, Desktop, Downloads, user Applications, app data, cloud, media, communications, developer, package, toolchain, container, and Trash buckets
description: Read-only focused storage bucket map for first-pass triage; buckets may overlap.
Read-only timestamped storage-map snapshots for before/after tracking and support reports.
Read-only before/after snapshot comparison showing storage bucket deltas.
Read-only inventory of cleanroom-created logs, snapshots, and recoverable Trash folders.

paths: Library, Mail, Messages, backups, Photos, cloud, browser profile, and Keychain access checks
description: Read-only access audit for macOS privacy-sensitive storage locations.

paths: Documents, Desktop, Downloads, screenshots, archives, installers, app data, and app bundles
description: Read-only personal storage review checklist that routes to focused commands.

paths: Top-level files and folders under ~/Documents or a provided folder
description: Review-only user-folder storage inventory with guard status and drill-down commands.
Review-only Desktop storage inventory with guard status and drill-down commands.

paths: Broken symlinks under Documents, Desktop, Downloads, Applications, or a provided folder
description: Read-only broken symlink inventory; cleanroom does not delete links.

paths: Quarantined files and apps under Downloads, Desktop, Documents, Applications, or a provided folder
description: Read-only macOS quarantine metadata inventory; cleanroom does not delete files or clear attributes.

paths: .DS_Store, AppleDouble ._* files, __MACOSX folders, Thumbs.db, Desktop.ini, and similar filesystem metadata clutter
description: Removes cross-platform filesystem metadata clutter from Desktop, Documents, Downloads, or paths passed to cleanroom metadata; metadata apply requires Trash mode.
Removes rebuildable Quick Look thumbnail and preview caches; macOS recreates previews as needed.
Removes rebuildable user font cache databases; actual font files remain protected.

paths: ~/Library/Caches/com.apple.Safari; Safari container caches; WebKit NetworkCache and WebContent caches
description: Removes Safari and WebKit cache folders only; history, cookies, bookmarks, passwords, website data, and profiles remain protected.
Removes saved app window/session restoration state; documents and app profile data remain protected.
Removes known rebuildable project cache artifacts while pruning .git, node_modules, and virtualenv folders.
Removes rebuildable Sparkle/Squirrel app updater cache and staging data while leaving apps, app profiles, and passwords protected.

paths: Screenshot and screen recording files under Desktop, Downloads, Documents, or a provided folder
description: Review-only screenshot inventory; screenshots can contain sensitive data.

paths: Archive and disk image files under Downloads, Desktop, Documents, or a provided folder
description: Review-only archive inventory; archives can be backups, deliverables, or installer sources.

paths: Android SDK, NDK, system images, AVDs, emulator, platform/build tools, and Studio caches
description: Review-only Android SDK and emulator inventory with safety labels and cleanup guidance.
Read-only user Login Items inventory; cleanroom does not disable, remove, or change Login Items.
Read-only vendor uninstaller and remover tool inventory; cleanroom does not run or remove these tools.

paths: Matching app bundles, vendor uninstallers, package receipts, and leftover candidates
description: Read-only app removal review workflow; cleanroom does not uninstall apps, run tools, unload services, or delete leftovers.

paths: Application Support, Containers, Group Containers, HTTPStorages, WebKit, and saved state
description: Review-only app data inventory; use leftovers or vendor tools before removing app state.

paths: iCloud Drive, CloudStorage, Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive, Box, and Sync
description: Review-only cloud-sync storage inventory; cleanroom does not remove synced folders.

paths: Large local files under iCloud Drive, CloudStorage, Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive, Box, and Sync
description: Read-only large local cloud-sync file inventory; cleanroom does not delete cloud files or evict local copies.

paths: Mail, Messages, Contacts, Calendars, Notes, Reminders, Voice Memos, and call history
description: Review-only personal app data inventory; cleanroom does not remove these protected stores.

paths: Mail, Mail Downloads, Messages, and Messages Attachments
description: Review-only communications storage inventory; cleanroom does not remove account, chat, or attachment data.
Removes log files older than seven days and empty log subfolders.
Removes old user diagnostic reports and CrashReporter files older than --days.
Removes rebuildable package manager stores.
Read-only macOS package installer receipt inventory; receipts are records, not uninstall targets.

paths: Go, pip, uv, Poetry, SwiftPM, Maven, Composer, RubyGems, and Bundler caches
description: Removes rebuildable language and toolchain caches that can be downloaded or rebuilt again.

paths: Chrome, Brave, Edge, Arc, Code, Cursor, Slack, Comet, Trae nested cache folders
description: Removes nested cache folders only; does not remove browser profiles, cookies, bookmarks, settings, or password data.
Removes stale node_modules folders under common personal project locations.

paths: Python virtualenv folders under Desktop/Documents/Downloads older than --days
description: Removes stale Python virtualenv folders only when they contain pyvenv.cfg.
Removes old downloaded .dmg, .pkg, .mpkg, .xip, .ipsw, and .iso installer files.

paths: Xcode DeviceSupport; Android NDK/system images; simulator state
description: Removes heavy SDK and simulator artifacts that may need reinstalling or resetting.
Asks tmutil to thin local snapshots; not recoverable through cleanroom Trash mode.

paths: ~/.gemini generated recordings, scratch, brain, implicit, conversations
description: Removes generated AI-agent workspace state and local conversation caches where known.

paths: ~/.lmstudio/models; ~/.ollama/models; Hugging Face and related model caches
description: Removes downloaded local AI model stores and backend extensions.

paths: ~/.colima; ~/.lima; Docker Desktop VM data; Podman storage
description: Removes local container VM disks, images, and volumes.
